what_it_does: The host HTML page for the 'Vi' UI kit (an AI workspace). It links the shared color/type tokens and the kit-local vi.css, mounts a #app root, loads React 18.3.1 + ReactDOM + Babel-standalone from a CDN, and then loads six text/babel kit component files (ViMark, ViStatusPill, ChatPanel, TaskTree, OutputPreview, ViKitApp) that assemble the workspace.
observations: Carries a @dsCard HTML comment with metadata: group 'UI Kit — Vi', name 'UI Kit — Vi (AI workspace)', subtitle 'Vi workspace — chat + task tree + live brochure preview', viewport 1480x800
Links two stylesheets: ../../colors_and_type.css (shared tokens) and vi.css (kit-local)
Loads React 18.3.1, ReactDOM, and @babel/standalone 7.29.0 from unpkg.com CDN with SRI integrity hashes and crossorigin
Loads six JSX files via <script type='text/babel' src=...> in a deliberate order ending with ViKitApp.jsx (the app assembler)
standouts: The @dsCard comment makes this page self-describing to the design-system gallery/catalogue tooling
Browser-Babel + CDN React, no build step — the kit runs directly in a browser
Explicit script load order with ViKitApp.jsx last (consistent with the components self-registering before assembly)
conventions: Follows the kit convention: shared token CSS + kit-local CSS, components as text/babel scripts, window-global registration assembled by an App file last
Follows the @dsCard self-describing-card convention for catalogue integration
Uses CDN dependencies with SRI hashes (a hardening convention)
concerns: Depends on external unpkg.com CDN at runtime — will not render offline or under a strict CSP that blocks external hosts
Uses React development builds rather than production builds
notes: The Vi AI-workspace kit's host page — token CSS + vi.css, CDN React/Babel, and six ordered JSX scripts assembling a chat + task-tree + live-preview workspace; self-describes via a @dsCard comment.
questions: Is the unpkg CDN dependency acceptable for the corpus's render/screenshot pipeline, or is a local/offline fallback expected?
Which of the six JSX files actually calls ReactDOM render into #app (presumably ViKitApp.jsx)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is the index/host page for the Vi kit and it wires exactly the stylesheets, runtime, and component scripts needed to render that workspace.
intention_signals: Aims to be the runnable entry point for the Vi AI-workspace kit, demonstrating a chat + task-tree + live-brochure-preview layout assembled from registered components on shared tokens.
novelty: The @dsCard metadata comment carrying gallery-card identity (group/name/subtitle/viewport) directly in the HTML.
contribution: Contributes the Vi kit's host/entry page: the @dsCard catalogue identity, the CSS+runtime wiring, and the ordered six-component assembly that produces the chat + task-tree + live-preview AI workspace.
summary_for_embedding: Host HTML for the Vi AI-workspace UI kit — links shared tokens and vi.css, loads CDN React/Babel, and assembles six text/babel components (chat, task tree, live preview) into #app, self-describing via a @dsCard comment.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the runnable entry page that assembles and renders the Vi AI-workspace kit for review.
